verses.dev: निवेश्य सेनां तु विभुः पद्भ्यां पादवतां वरः।अभिगन्तुं स काकुत्थ्समियेष गुरुवर्तकम्
verses.trans: On encamping the army, lord Bharata, the best among men, wished to approach on foot Rama who was devoted to his father.

verses.dev: निविष्टमात्रे सैन्ये तु यथोद्देशं विनीतवत्।भरतो भ्रातरं वाक्यं शत्रुघ्नमिदमब्रवीत्
verses.trans: After the army encamped in the assigned places, Bharata said to his brother Satrughna thus:

verses.dev: क्षिप्रं वनमिदं सौम्य नरसङ्घै स्समन्ततः।लुब्धैश्च सहितैरेभि स्त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि
verses.trans: O dear Satrughna, you should explore this forest quickly along with groups of our people and hunters as well.

words.m: in the company of hunters as well

verses.trans: Let Guha, surrounded by a thousand kinsmen and armed with bows, arrows and swords look for Rama.

words.m: armed with bows, arrows and swords

verses.dev: अमात्यै स्सह पौरैश्च गुरुभिश्च द्विजातिभिः।वनं सर्वं चरिष्यामि पद्भ्यां परिवृत स्स्वयम्
verses.trans: 'I myself shall go on foot surrounded by ministers, citizens, preceptors and brahmins and search the entire forest.

verses.dev: यावन्न रामं द्रक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मणं वा महाबलम्।वैदेहीं च महाभागां न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति
verses.trans: 'I shall not rest in peace until I see Rama, powerful Lakshmana and illustrious princess of Videha (Sita).

verses.dev: यावन्न चन्द्रसङ्काशं द्रक्ष्यामि शुभमाननम्। भ्रातुः पद्मपलाशाक्षं न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति
verses.trans: I shall not attain peace until I see my brother, Rama whose auspicious countenance looks radiant as the Moon and whose eyes as lotuspetals.

verses.dev: यावन्न चरणौ भ्रातुः पार्थिवव्यञ्जनान्वितौ।शिरसा धारयिष्यामि न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति
verses.trans: Peace shall not come to me until I hold on my head the feet of my brother, the insignia of royalty.

verses.dev: यावन्न राज्ये राज्यार्हः पितृपैतामहे स्थितः।अभिषेकजलक्लिन्नो न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति
verses.trans: I shall not attain peace until Rama, who is worthy of this kingdom, is sprinkled with the holy water of coronation and assumes the ancestral kingdom.

words.m: one who is worthy of this kingdom
wet with the holy waters of consecration
pertaining to our father and forefathers

verses.dev: सिद्धार्थः खलु सौमित्रिर्यश्चन्द्रविमलोपमम्।मुखं पश्यति रामस्य राजीवाक्षं महाद्युति
verses.trans: Lakshmana, one who beholds the highly effulgent countenance of Rama, resembling the spotless Moon and whose eyes resemble red lotuses, is blessed indeed

verses.dev: कृतकृत्या महाभागा वैदेही जनकात्मजा।भर्तारं सागरान्तायाः पृथिव्या यानुगच्छति
verses.trans: Sita, the illustrious daughter of Janaka and princess of Videha, has accomplished her purpose by following Rama, the lord of this earth bounded by the ocean.

verses.dev: सुभगश्चित्रकूटोऽसौ गिरिराजोपमो गिरिः।यस्मिन्वसति काकुत्स्थः कुबेर इव नन्दने
verses.trans: Like Kubera in the Nandana garden, Rama lives here on mount Chitrakuta which is indeed blessed like the Himalaya, king of the mountains.

words.m: comparable to the king of the mountains, Himavan

verses.dev: कृतकार्यमिदं दुर्गं वनं व्यालनिषेवितम्।यदध्यास्ते महातेजा राम श्शस्त्रभृतां वरः
verses.trans: Fortunate is this inaccessible forest which is inhabited by wild animals to have become the dwelling place of the most radiant Rama, the foremost among the wielders of weapons.

verses.dev: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा भरतः पुरषर्षभः।पद्भ्यामेव महाबाहुः प्रविवेश महाद्वनम्
verses.trans: Having said thus, the best of men, mightyarmed and highly energetic Bharata entered the great forest on foot.

verses.dev: स तानि द्रुमजालानि जातानि गिरिसानुषु।पुष्पिताग्राणि मध्येन जगाम वदतां वरः
verses.trans: Bharata, the best among the eloquent, made his way amidst multitude of trees grown on mountain slopes with their tops in full bloom.

words.m: through the midst of multitude of trees

verses.trans: Having reached a flowering sala tree atop Chitrakuta mountain, he beheld a lofty banner of smoke rising from the fire burning in Rama's hermitage.

verses.dev: तं दृष्ट्वा भरत श्रीमान्मुमोद सह बान्धवः।अत्र राम इति ज्ञात्वा गतः पारमिवाम्भसः
verses.trans: On seeing the smoke, majestic Bharata in the company of relatives concluded that Rama was staying there and rejoiced like a man who had reached the other bank of the river.

verses.dev: स चित्रकूटे तु गिरौ निशम्यव रामाश्रमं पुण्यजनोपपन्नम्।गुहेन सार्धं त्वरितो जगाम पुनर्निवेश्यैव चमूं महात्मा
verses.trans: Having heard of Rama's hermitage on the Chitrakuta mountain inhabited by pious men, magnanimous Bharata, commanded his army to stay at a distance, and quickly proceeded with Guha. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे अष्टनवतितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the ninetyeighth sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
